synthesis.summary: CF Threat Watch flagged a brute-force-suspect pattern (401×37 then 40×2xx SUCCESS logins) from a single IP on ft-crm.com. Joachim acknowledged instantly; escalation to the security on-call (Max, Agustín) had paging misses (Max unavailable, Agustín's escalation expired) but both joined the channel manually within ~4 minutes anyway via Stephen Riolo, who took over as lead. Team is jointly investigating via Cloudflare security analytics; current read is a shared-network false positive, not an attack — CF itself classifies it as 'not an exploit'.
key_events: 16 Jul 12:10:44 UTC alert fires, Joachim acknowledges within seconds

12:11:36-12:14:59 UTC escalation to security on-call: Max paged but unavailable, Agustín's escalation expired, Stephen Riolo acknowledges and becomes lead; Max and Agustín both join the channel regardless

12:21-12:39 UTC joint investigation via Cloudflare dashboard; Agustín: 'doesn't look like an attack and CF is classifying it as not an exploit'; Stephen: 'don't think this is an attack... more some shared network'
Still actively investigating at check time (~29min old)
16 Jul 23:42 CEST bot nudge asking to mark resolved -- no human response yet (overnight, expected)
proposals: Verify via Cloudflare security analytics + bot session double-check - in progress
synthesis.why: Fast ack and correct B6 security escalation flow (SOC→SRE→security team), even though the paging itself misfired (Max unavailable, Agustín's escalation expired) — both engaged within ~4 minutes regardless. Too fresh and too well-handled to flag; current evidence points to a false positive.

synthesis.summary: Leonbets activity-manager and activity-manager-lifecycles consumer groups were spamming slow-processing alerts (SOC noise), traced by Matteo to a shared Aurora writer being saturated by full-brand config reloads (leonbets has 598k actions, ~7.9M rows read per pod per reload). Matteo deployed a temporary kubectl-level fix (scoped action loading + longer reload interval), cutting RDS CPU from 94-99% to 40-50% and lag from ~94k/77k to <2k on each group within ~90 minutes of investigation starting. Set to Monitoring at 12:16:59 CEST. As of 17 Jul morning, Matteo confirms PR #490 is performing well (RDS CPU healthy, lag under 5-6k peak over the past 12h) and will move it out of draft for review; Malcolm (given the logic overhaul) is asked to review next week.
key_events: 16 Jul 10:47 UTC alert fires (pair of alerts, 2 related incidents auto-merged)
12:47-13:20 CEST Matteo root-causes: DB saturation from unscoped config reload, not a Kafka/consumer issue; two prior scale-up attempts (partitions, pod count) made it worse
13:20-14:09 CEST Matteo drafts and deploys PR #490 (scoped action loading) as a kubectl-level change, plus bumped lifecycles reload interval to 300s
14:16:59 CEST incident updated with full root-cause writeup and measured impact; status → Monitoring
Explicit note in the update: today's kubectl-level changes revert on the next OneClick deploy unless PR #490 is merged and the env vars persisted
No new human activity this cycle (bot nudge only) -- PR #490 merge / EV persistence still not confirmed done
17 Jul 10:03-10:04 CEST Matteo: PR #490 performing well over past 12h (no alerts, lag <5-6k), moving out of draft; will ask Malcolm to review 'next week' given it's a logic overhaul
17 Jul 12:33 CEST: Matteo downgrades severity P2→P3 as the situation stays stable; status remains Monitoring
proposals: Scoped action loading (PR #490) - applied via kubectl today, durable merge+EV persistence still pending
Lifecycles reload interval 120s→300s - applied, durable EV persistence still pending
open_breaches: A2: durable fix (PR #490 merge + EV persistence) still not merged -- review now scheduled for 'next week', so today's kubectl-level changes remain exposed to reverting on the next OneClick deploy until then
synthesis.why: Real progress -- PR is proven effective over 12h and moving to review -- but merge is now explicitly pushed to 'next week', extending the window where the fix could revert on a deploy.
synthesis.suggested_action: Ask Malcolm to prioritize reviewing PR #490 sooner than 'next week' given the revert-on-deploy risk; merge and persist the two EV settings as soon as approved.

synthesis.summary: Fusion API 401 errors blocking on-site notifications/Rich Inbox for Spinbet Terragon -- 2nd occurrence of this alert family in a week (see INC-12617). Following Andrea's direction yesterday, Matteo fully root-caused it this morning: the partner's frontends send brand slug 'spinbetterragon' (no hyphen) which matches neither of FT's accepted values ('spinbet-terragon' or the 'vegastars' alias), so 100% of Pusher auth requests 401 -- traffic jumped ~20x on 8 Jul, likely an operator frontend release. Matteo then sent a fully corrected, technically accurate partner message to #ftcrm-mtm at 10:29 CEST explaining the mismatch and asking the partner to confirm the definitive brand slug before FT changes its config.

synthesis.summary: Stanleybet Romania (partner) reported users showing as 'unsubscribed' in activity reporting despite being subscribed. Giancarlo confirmed within the hour it's a false alarm: automated email-security scanners/link-prefetchers auto-POST the RFC 8058 one-click-unsubscribe link while scanning inbound mail (no human click), which Stanleybet's self-hosted unsubscribe page treats as a real opt-out and forwards to prisma-api, which in turn feeds the reporting pipeline (falsely flagging 74,234 brand-206 users) while leaving real consent untouched (confirmed against the operator, Comtrade, which still shows opted-in). Fix PR #106 (gate the legacy handler so the enriched/authoritative consent path wins) is in review. Partner has independently confirmed on their side that they see no real unsubscribe problem, only the reporting glitch.
key_events: 15 Jul 13:07 UTC partner (Marius) first reports the mismatch via Intercom support
16 Jul 12:41 UTC Tara (support) escalates to SRE after internal checks
16 Jul 12:51-13:47 CEST Giancarlo investigates, finds it's a reporting false-positive (consent-direction check bug), still pending final partner confirmation
16 Jul 14:47 CEST Andrea flags a possible pattern link to INC-13738 (Betiro no-unsubs) and a separate informal Slack discussion
16 Jul 16:33 CEST Incident Lead handed over Joachim → James Hodgson

16 Jul 15:02 CEST partner (Marius) confirms: 'seeing how we are managing the unsubscribe function ourselves, I don't think it's an issue. The only issue we're seeing is the faulty reporting' — partner side fully satisfied
16 Jul 23:25 CEST Incident Lead handed over James Hodgson -> Gerard E. Pelayo (no substantive activity since)

17 Jul 08:12-08:16 CEST Giancarlo confirms false alarm; posts a full end-to-end technical write-up (scanner/prefetch auto-POST -> prisma-api hardcodes opt-out -> conversion-service misreads it as email_unsubscribed) with PR #106 already in review, plus data-backfill and upstream-hardening follow-ups noted
17 Jul 08:15 CEST Incident Lead handed to Joachim; 08:37 CEST formal incident.io update posted with the full root-cause summary

17 Jul 09:48-12:38 CEST Giancarlo checks with the partner (Marius) about deploying the fix now vs waiting; partner asks for older reports to be corrected too (told: fix is forward-only, values were miswritten not just misread) and can't replicate on their end to verify pre-deploy; both agree to deploy Monday rather than risk a change going into the weekend

promises: Root cause (false-positive reporting bug) explained internally 16 Jul; partner has now separately confirmed they see no real issue on their end, only the reporting glitch
Fix deploy jointly agreed for Monday (weekend-safety call), not immediate -- deliberate, communicated decision

proposals: Fix the consent-event opt-in/opt-out check so it stops miscounting as unsubscribe — root cause identified, deploy scheduled Monday by mutual agreement with the partner
synthesis.why: Textbook handling -- root cause traced precisely end-to-end with quantified impact, a fix ready, real consent confirmed untouched, and the deploy timing (Monday, not this weekend) was an explicit informed decision (D12) made jointly with the partner rather than either side unilaterally delaying.

INC-12246.name: Exploitable Image found in Lottomart Privileged Kubernetes Container

synthesis.summary: Wiz found a privileged, internet-facing Kubernetes container (ambassador) in Lottomart production with exploitable CVEs (vulnerable curl dependency). Root cause identified fast (04-06 Jul) and a fix (PR #20, replace curl with Python) was tested and rolled out to Lottomart by 06 Jul. Patrik explicitly asked that the fix be rolled out to all other brands too ('close those also if there are new ones and we release and update to all'). The last substantive update was 08 Jul ('rolling out the fixed image on this PV') — since then, 9 days of complete silence in the channel, still shown as P1/Investigating, with no confirmation the rollout ever reached brands beyond Lottomart.
key_events: 03 Jul 21:29 UTC incident opened from a Wiz finding, escalated same night, Joachim → Agustín as lead
06 Jul 06:25 UTC formal update: root cause (curl CVEs) confirmed, PR #20 opened, plan to test then roll out Lottomart-first then other brands
04 Jul: Patrik explicitly asks that once released, similar CVEs on other brands also get closed out as part of the same rollout
08 Jul 07:17-07:18 CEST: Agustín says the fixed image is being rolled out on 'this PV' (a stable-platform-version release train) — last substantive message
08 Jul 07:48/09:18 CEST: two automated check-ins asking if it can be marked resolved — no human reply
17 Jul 09:22 CEST: Andrea Azzopardi joins the channel (passive join only, no content) — first activity in 9 days
proposals: Replace curl with Python in Ambassador image (PR #20) — tested and deployed to Lottomart 08 Jul; rollout to remaining brands never confirmed
open_breaches: B6/A2: P1 security incident silent for 9 days with the incident.io record still 'Investigating' — no evidence the Lottomart-only fix was ever extended to other brands as Patrik asked
D15: the incident record itself only has 2 status updates (03 Jul, 06 Jul) and doesn't reflect the 08 Jul rollout message or current state at all — not understandable from the record alone
synthesis.why: A genuine (not false-positive) P1 security exposure has gone silent for 9 days with no confirmation the fix was rolled out beyond Lottomart, despite Patrik explicitly asking for full-brand closure — this is exactly the B6/A2 pattern management wants surfaced, not left to quietly age.
synthesis.suggested_action: Get Agustín (or current owner — Andrea just joined the channel today) to confirm rollout status across all brands and post a closing update, or formally re-baseline the incident with current state.

synthesis.summary: Cocobet (Mystake) blocked users appearing to 'unblock' themselves. Joachim ran a thorough data trace (event tables vs canonical snapshots) and confirmed the true root cause: Cocobet's daily reconciliation feed doesn't include users who should remain blocked, so FT's ~04:20 CEST recon job flips them back to unblocked. Sent the partner a clear, specific technical explanation and ask (check their recon feed setup); incident.io moved Investigating → Waiting on Partner. Partner initially pushed back (claiming 0 blocked users), but Joachim continued the data trace and pinpointed 2 specific users being wrongly unblocked today by the recon service, confirming the mechanism precisely.
key_events: 17 Jul 08:42 UTC incident opened (Intercom ticket), Joachim takes lead instantly
10:54-11:51 CEST Joachim traces block/unblock event history at the data level, rules out a CRM-side bug
12:18-12:19 CEST Joachim sends partner a precise technical writeup identifying the daily recon job (cron 04:20 CEST) as the mechanism and asks them to check their recon feed setup
12:31 CEST incident update posted, status → Waiting on Partner
08:57-09:09 UTC partner (Sandro) pushes back, says 0 blocked users on their segment; Joachim keeps investigating rather than dropping it
10:17 UTC Joachim confirms precisely: 2 users (57329598, 57389211) blocked today and both flipped back to unblocked by the reconciliation service, matching the partner's own example (57381714)

promises: root-cause explanation and recon-feed check request — delivered 12:18-12:19 CEST
precise data confirmation of the affected users — delivered 10:17 UTC after partner pushback

synthesis.why: Rigorous, fast root-cause work (P1, ~4h from open to a precise partner-side diagnosis) with clear, specific comms to the partner, and Joachim didn't back down when the partner initially disputed it -- kept tracing until the exact affected users were confirmed. No breaches, well handled.
